pi_items.source: Verified against task description and live environment testing. All required BIM operators confirmed available: draw_polyline_wall, draw_polyline_slab, add_door, generate_space, generate_spaces_from_walls, save_project (evidence: 10_available_bim_operators.txt from live test on 2026-03-20). Each skill maps to a concrete task requirement.

All tags independently verified. IFC4 schema confirmed from live hierarchy test (evidence: 14_ifc_hierarchy_verification.txt) and buildingSMART IFC4 standard (https://standards.buildingsmart.org/IFC/DEV/IFC4_2/FINAL/HTML/). Long horizon: 300 max_steps is 50% more than the previous maximum of 200 in blenderbim_env. From-scratch: setup creates blank IFC project via bpy.ops.bim.create_project(). Commercial architecture: 12m x 8m = 96 sq m is a realistic small retail unit per UK retail size data (https://shepcom.com/theaveragesizeofaretailshop/). AEC: matches buildingSMART's domain.
BIM modeling of commercial retail buildings is standard architectural practice. BlenderBIM/Bonsai is specifically designed for architects and BIM practitioners (https://bonsaibim.org/).
IFC is the open standard for AEC industry interoperability per buildingSMART International (https://www.buildingsmart.org/standards/bsi-standards/industry-foundation-classes/).

Confirmed from export_result.sh line 7. Export script tested in live environment — produces valid JSON for both file-exists and file-missing cases (evidence: 11_export_result_no_file.json, 12_export_result_with_ifc.json).

Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 11: rm -f), export_result.sh (line 16: ifc_path), and task description. BIMProjects directory confirmed to exist with ga:ga ownership and write permission (evidence: 07_bimprojects_dir_listing.txt). Stale file properly cleared at task start.

Task description: 4 external walls (200mm thick perimeter) + 2 internal partition walls (100mm thick) = 6 minimum. IfcWall is a standard IFC4 entity (https://standards.buildingsmart.org/IFC/RELEASE/IFC4_3/HTML/lexical/IfcWall.htm). Export script counts via ifcopenshell ifc.by_type('IfcWall').

Task description: 1 ground floor slab + 1 flat roof slab = 2 minimum. IfcSlab is standard IFC4 (https://standards.buildingsmart.org/IFC/RELEASE/IFC4_3/HTML/lexical/IfcSlab.htm). Export script counts via ifc.by_type('IfcSlab').

Task description: 1 external entrance door (1.0m x 2.1m on south wall) + 1 internal door (0.9m x 2.1m between retail and storage) = 2 minimum. Dimensions are realistic standard commercial door sizes. Export script counts via ifc.by_type('IfcDoor').

Task description: 3 rooms — retail area (~60 sq m), storage room (~20 sq m), staff WC (~4 sq m). Room areas sum to ~84 sq m out of 96 sq m total footprint (12m x 8m); remaining ~12 sq m accounted for by wall thickness — geometrically consistent. IfcSpace is standard IFC4 (https://github.com/buildingSMART/IFC4.3.x-development/blob/master/docs/schemas/core/IfcProductExtension/Entities/IfcSpace.md).

Descriptive LongName values specified in task description. Export script collects IfcSpace.LongName attributes (line 92). IfcSpace.LongName is a standard IFC4 attribute for human-readable room names. Agent may use these exact names or reasonable variants.

Single-storey building. Setup creates exactly one IfcBuildingStorey via bpy.ops.bim.create_project(). Live test confirmed hierarchy contains exactly one IfcBuildingStorey named 'My Storey' (evidence: 14_ifc_hierarchy_verification.txt). Export script counts via ifc.by_type('IfcBuildingStorey').
Design parameter consistent with other blenderbim_env tasks (range 60-80). Verifier is a stub; actual evaluation via VLM checklist with 100 total points across 9 completion items.

privileged_info_summary: The agent must model a single-storey commercial retail unit (12m x 8m footprint, 3.2m floor-to-ceiling height) starting from a blank IFC4 project in BlenderBIM (Bonsai 0.8.5-alpha on Blender 4.2.4 LTS). The initial spatial hierarchy uses Bonsai's default names: IfcProject 'My Project' > IfcSite 'My Site' > IfcBuilding 'My Building' > IfcBuildingStorey 'My Storey' at elevation 0.0. Required elements: (1) 4 external IfcWall elements (200mm thick) forming a closed rectangular perimeter, plus 2 internal IfcWall partitions (100mm thick) dividing the interior into 3 rooms — minimum 6 IfcWall total; (2) 2 IfcSlab elements: 1 ground floor slab and 1 flat roof slab at 3.2m; (3) 2 IfcDoor elements: 1 external entrance (1.0m x 2.1m, south wall) and 1 internal door (0.9m x 2.1m, retail-to-storage); (4) 3 IfcSpace elements with LongName set to 'Retail Area' (~60 sq m), 'Storage Room' (~20 sq m), and 'Staff WC' (~4 sq m). All elements must be contained within the building storey. The final file must be saved as /home/ga/BIMProjects/retail_unit.ifc. Key BIM operators available: draw_polyline_wall, draw_polyline_slab, add_door, generate_space, generate_spaces_from_walls, save_project. F3 operator search is available as fallback. The BIMProjects directory is at /home/ga/BIMProjects/ with ga:ga ownership. No pre-existing data needed — this is a from-scratch modeling task with 300 max steps.
